key_insight: AI is turning nanoparticle electron microscopy from manual image interpretation into a quantitative platform for structural, dynamic, and scientific inference.
engineering_relevance: For materials discovery, these methods can speed nanoparticle characterization, connect structure to properties, and support closed-loop experimentation for faster design decisions.
method: Uses ML/DL for particle detection, segmentation, and morphology quantification in TEM/STEM/HRTEM data.
Applies advanced models such as transformers, self-supervised learning, foundation models, and physics-informed learning.
Combines microscopy with simulations, metadata, and multimodal inputs to infer 2D-to-3D structure and in situ dynamics.
evidence_signal: The review synthesizes methodologies across multiple microscopy modes and explicitly evaluates their advantages, limitations, benchmarking needs, and data requirements.
watch_item: Performance depends strongly on high-quality labeled data, robust benchmarking, and careful validation before deployment in autonomous workflows.
paper_title: The evolution of AI from image interpretation toward scientific inference in nanoparticle electron microscopy
